action.reasoning: Observation: Starting fresh. Analysis: The task is robot exploration - select neighboring nodes to maximize coverage. The environment provides nodes with utility values (frontier cells). A greedy utility-based planner should work well as baseline. I'll start with a heuristic approach: always pick the highest-utility neighbor, with tie-breaking by distance. This doesn't require training but should score well. I'll also implement a more sophisticated approach with lookahead/backtracking. Decision: Build heuristic planner first, test it, then submit eval to get baseline score and timing.
